title: Donatello, David (quiz)
topic_path: Sculpture and architecture in central Italy

assessment_items.question_content:
**David, the biblical underdog, defeated his giant enemy with God's help. Who identified with David?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Both the Florentines and the Medici family identified with the figure of David. The Florentines identified with David because they defeated the powerful Duke of Milan in the early fifteenth century. The Medici family identified with David and the king that he would become.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--david--bronze--c--1440s)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**[[☃ dropdown 1]] made figures look like they were moving and really alive, unlike medieval sculpture.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31949c3efa6d6672570409df2783dfdd.jpg)
hints:
**David stands in a contrapposto pose, which is a relaxed pose with one leg straight and the other bent. This pose comes from classical (ancient Greek and Roman) sculpture.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--david--bronze--c--1440s)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Donatello's David?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The eroticism of Donatello's *David* is unusual for its time. The biblical text does not suggest that the event was a sexually charged one.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--david--bronze--c--1440s)**

choices.content: Donatello's David was the first free-standing classical nude since antiquity.
Donatello's David shows a revival of the classical interest in and respect for the body.
This sculpture is made of bronze and was made with the lost wax method, which is the same method that ancient sculptors used.
The sculpture is typical of its time in that it shows a sense of eroticism.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This sculpture shows a return to the admiration of the human body we see in ancient Greek and Roman art.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Medieval artists and theologians saw the human body as corrupt, but Early Renaissance artists adopted a different, classical view of the body that allowed them the celebrate it in art.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--david--bronze--c--1440s)**
